---
title: "1.1.1 Binary represents data"
subtitle: "Data Representation P2-P3"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal p { margin-top: 0.4em; margin-bottom: 0.25em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.14em; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 1 Directory / 返回 Unit 1 目录](../../index.html)

## Why Binary?

### The basic building block of all computers

The <span class="term">binary number system</span> (二进制) is chosen because it only consists of <span class="mark">$1$s and $0$s</span>. Since computers contain millions and millions of tiny <span class="term">switches</span> (开关), which must be in the <span class="mark">ON</span> or <span class="mark">OFF</span> position, they can be represented by the binary system:

::: {.tight-list}
- a switch in the <span class="mark">ON</span> position is represented by <span class="mark">$1$</span>
- a switch in the <span class="mark">OFF</span> position is represented by <span class="mark">$0$</span>
:::

## Storing and Processing Data

### Logic gates and binary format

Switches used in a computer make use of <span class="term">logic gates</span> (逻辑门) and are used to <span class="mark">store</span> and <span class="mark">process data</span>.

Any form of data needs to be converted into a <span class="mark">binary format</span> so that it can be processed by the computer.

No matter how complex the system, the basic building block in all computers is the binary number system.

## Classroom Check

### Understand why and how binary works

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- that computers use <span class="term">binary</span> because electronic switches have <span class="mark">two states (ON/OFF)</span>, represented by <span class="mark">$1$ and $0$</span>
- that computers contain millions and millions of tiny <span class="term">switches</span> which can be represented by binary
- that a switch in the <span class="mark">ON</span> position is represented by <span class="mark">$1$</span> and a switch in the <span class="mark">OFF</span> position is represented by <span class="mark">$0$</span>
- that switches in a computer make use of <span class="term">logic gates</span> (逻辑门) and are used to <span class="mark">store and process data</span>
- that <span class="mark">any form of data</span> must be converted into binary format before the computer can process it
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Binary, Denary and Hexadecimal

The next deck covers the three number systems used in computing and how to convert between them.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 1 Directory / 返回 Unit 1 目录](../../index.html)
